verses.1: For the music director. A Psalm of David, when Nathan the prophet came to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba. Be gracious to me, God, according to Your faithfulness; According to the greatness of Your compassion, wipe out my wrongdoings.
verses.2: Wash me thoroughly from my guilt And cleanse me from my sin.
verses.3: For I know my wrongdoings, And my sin is constantly before me.
verses.4: Against You, You only, I have sinned And done what is evil in Your sight, So that You are justified when You speak And blameless when You judge.
verses.5: Behold, I was brought forth in guilt, And in sin my mother conceived me.
verses.6: Behold, You desire truth in the innermost being, And in secret You will make wisdom known to me.
verses.7: Purify me with hyssop, and I will be clean; Cleanse me, and I will be whiter than snow.
verses.8: Let me hear joy and gladness, Let the bones You have broken rejoice.
verses.9: Hide Your face from my sins And wipe out all my guilty deeds.
verses.10: Create in me a clean heart, God, And renew a steadfast spirit within me.
verses.11: Do not cast me away from Your presence, And do not take Your Holy Spirit from me.
verses.12: Restore to me the joy of Your salvation, And sustain me with a willing spirit.
verses.13: Then I will teach wrongdoers Your ways, And sinners will be converted to You.
verses.14: Save me from the guilt of bloodshed, God, the God of my salvation; Then my tongue will joyfully sing of Your righteousness.
verses.15: Lord, open my lips, So that my mouth may declare Your praise.
verses.16: For You do not delight in sacrifice, otherwise I would give it; You do not take pleasure in burnt offering.
verses.17: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; A broken and a contrite heart, God, You will not despise.
verses.18: By Your favor do good to Zion; Build the walls of Jerusalem.
verses.19: Then You will delight in righteous sacrifices, In burnt offering and whole burnt offering; Then bulls will be offered on Your altar.
